studies.name: Contrast Echocardiogram
studies.preparation: IV access; assess renal function if contrast used.
studies.notes: Contrast-enhanced echo; specify indication (e.g. palpitations, murmur).

studies.name: Exercise Stress Test
studies.preparation: Light meal 2 hours prior; comfortable shoes.

studies.name: Full Pulmonary Function Test
studies.preparation: No heavy meal 2 hours prior; follow lab instructions.
studies.notes: Complete PFT with lung volumes and diffusion as indicated.

studies.name: Holter Monitor (24 hours)
studies.preparation: Avoid showering while wearing monitor if instructed.

studies.name: Holter Monitor (48 hours)
studies.preparation: Avoid showering while wearing monitor if instructed.

studies.name: Pulmonary Function Test (Spirometry)
studies.preparation: No bronchodilator before test unless ordered; comfortable clothing.

studies.name: Stress Echocardiogram
studies.notes: Exercise or pharmacologic stress with echo imaging.

studies.name: Sleep Study (Polysomnography)
studies.preparation: Avoid caffeine/alcohol per lab; bring sleepwear.
studies.notes: Overnight sleep study for OSA and related disorders.

studies.name: Myocardial Perfusion (Persantine)
studies.preparation: NPO 4 hours; caffeine restrictions per lab.

studies.name: Myocardial Perfusion Imaging (Nuclear Stress)
studies.preparation: NPO 4 hours; caffeine restrictions per lab.

studies.name: Rest MUGA (Ventricular Function)
studies.preparation: No special prep; pregnancy test if applicable.

studies.name: Abdominal X-ray
studies.preparation: Remove clothing over abdomen; may need to hold breath.
studies.notes: 2 views (supine/upright); for GI issues like obstruction.

studies.name: Ankle X-ray
studies.notes: Specify right, left, or bilateral in ordering note.

studies.name: Cervical Spine X-ray
studies.preparation: Remove necklaces and metal from neck.

studies.name: Chest X-ray
studies.preparation: Remove upper body clothing and jewelry; wear gown.
studies.notes: PA and lateral views; for respiratory symptoms or screening.

studies.name: Elbow X-ray
studies.notes: Specify right, left, or bilateral in ordering note.

studies.name: Extremity X-ray
studies.preparation: Remove clothing/jewelry from affected limb; immobilize as needed.
studies.notes: Example for knee (3 views); specify body part in order.

studies.name: Foot X-ray
studies.notes: Specify right, left, or bilateral in ordering note.

studies.name: Hip X-ray
studies.notes: Specify right, left, or bilateral in ordering note.

studies.name: Shoulder X-ray
studies.notes: Specify right, left, or bilateral in ordering note.

studies.name: Sinuses X-ray
studies.notes: Paranasal sinuses; specify side if unilateral.

studies.name: Wrist X-ray
studies.notes: Specify right, left, or bilateral in ordering note.

studies.name: Abdomen + Pelvis Ultrasound
studies.preparation: NPO 6–8 hours; full bladder for pelvic views if required.
studies.notes: Combined abdominal and pelvic survey incl. reproductive organs.

studies.name: Abdominal Ultrasound
studies.preparation: NPO 6-8 hours prior; drink water if needed for bladder.
studies.notes: Complete exam; for organ evaluation like liver/kidneys.

studies.name: Arterial Doppler Lower Extremity (ABI)
studies.notes: Ankle-brachial index and arterial flow; specify side.

studies.name: Bladder Ultrasound
studies.preparation: Full bladder unless post-void residual ordered.
studies.notes: Bladder volume and post-void residual when indicated.

studies.name: Female Pelvis Ultrasound (incl. Transvaginal)
studies.preparation: Empty bladder for transvaginal; follow lab protocol.

studies.name: Male Pelvis Ultrasound
studies.notes: Prostate and pelvic organs; excludes transrectal.

studies.name: Musculoskeletal Ultrasound - Ankle
studies.preparation: Expose ankle; specify side in note.
studies.notes: Achilles tendon, plantar fascia, or ankle soft tissue.

studies.name: Musculoskeletal Ultrasound - Knee
studies.preparation: Expose knee; specify side in note.
studies.notes: Knee soft tissue and effusion assessment.

studies.name: Musculoskeletal Ultrasound - Shoulder
studies.preparation: Expose shoulder; specify side in note.
studies.notes: Rotator cuff and soft tissue shoulder evaluation.

studies.name: Obstetric Ultrasound (First Trimester)
studies.preparation: Full bladder early pregnancy if transabdominal.

studies.name: Pelvic Ultrasound
studies.preparation: Drink 32 oz water 1 hour prior for full bladder; may undress.

studies.name: Renal Ultrasound
studies.preparation: Hydration; may need full bladder for some views.

studies.name: Thyroid Ultrasound
studies.notes: Soft tissue neck; for nodules or enlargement.

studies.name: Venous Doppler Upper Extremity
studies.notes: Upper extremity DVT evaluation; specify side.

studies.name: Mammography
studies.preparation: No deodorant, powder, powder; wear two-piece clothing; bring priors.
studies.notes: Bilateral screening; for breast cancer detection.

studies.name: Targeted Breast Ultrasound
studies.notes: Focal breast lesion evaluation; specify side.

studies.name: Bone Density Scan
studies.preparation: Avoid calcium supplements 24 hours prior; no recent barium exams.
studies.notes: Axial skeleton; for osteoporosis risk assessment.

studies.name: Abdomen/Pelvis CT
studies.preparation: NPO 4 hours; arrive early for oral contrast if ordered.
studies.notes: With contrast; for detailed abdominal evaluation.

studies.name: Chest CT (Noncontrast)
studies.preparation: Remove metal from chest; fasting not required.

studies.name: CT Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast
studies.preparation: NPO 4 hours; oral contrast per protocol.

studies.name: CT Head with Contrast
studies.preparation: NPO 4 hours; check renal function and allergies.

studies.name: Head CT (Noncontrast)
studies.notes: For acute neurological issues; avoid if pregnant.

studies.name: Brain MRI
studies.preparation: Screen for metal implants/claustrophobia; remove metal objects.
studies.notes: With and without contrast; for brain pathology.

studies.name: Lumbar Spine MRI
studies.preparation: Screen for contraindications; may take 30-60 min.

studies.name: MRI Brain with Contrast
studies.preparation: MRI safety screening; renal function if gadolinium.
